observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile, determine the nature of transactions, record the
necessary adjusting entries, and prepare the BRS for DBP accounts of the LGU
to report an accurate Cash in Bank – LCCA balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to properly record and classify transactions to the appropriate
accounts, and review and reconcile the amounts and entries made in the
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the details disclosed in the Notes to FS
to report accurately the transactions and information for the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require (a) the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their records to reflect
the actual utilization and unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund and make
adjusting entries, if warranted; (b) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and/or cancelled
checks, if any; and (c) SDOs to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the
purpose is served.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to trace the transactions of the account to substantiate the
account balance and record the liquidation pertaining the fund transfer from
DILG.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of
accounts to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to review the balances of the accounts to determine the actual
liability to the agencies concerned and prepare appropriate adjusting entries
for the fair presentation of the accounts in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the lacking disbursement
vouchers with complete supporting documents and adhere to the prescribed
timeline in the submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and documents.

1.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their respective
records on the allotments and obligations regularly. Moreover, direct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the SCBAA pursuant to
paragraph 14 of PPSAS 24.

2.20 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require
the Municipal Accountant to (a) record the refund from contractors; (b)
prepare adjusting entries to revert back the stale and cancelled checks; and
(c) prepare the monthly and year-end Bank Reconciliation Statements and
maintain Subsidiary Ledger for each bank account.

3.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of PPEs and prepare
the necessary report (RPCPPE), and the Supply Officer to reconcile the
balances in the PEC with the balances per books of the Municipal
Accountant; and

b. Municipal Accountant to (a) prepare adjusting entries for the transfer
of PPE from Trust Fund books to the General Fund; (b) reclassify the
PPEs to its proper accounts; and (c) prepare entries to record the result
of the reconciliation between the Supply and Accounting Office.

4.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to (1) trace and verify the status and history of these
construction in progress accounts and prepare the Subsidiary Ledger for
every project; (2) prepare journal entry to reclassify the completed
projects from the CIP to the appropriate PPE account and recognize the
corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of completion; and
(3) adjust the procured materials delivered to the barangays.

5.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (1) HRMO to
review the entries in leave cards to reflect actual leave credits balances; and
(2) Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to report the accurate
amount of Terminal Leave Benefits and Leave Benefits Payable accounts in
the financial statements.

6.16 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their
records on DRRMF to present the correct amounts in the Notes to FS and
SAAOB;

b. Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the subsidiary ledger of unexpended
DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer and (2) transfer the amount that
was erroneously drawn to the General Fund and STF bank accounts; and

c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the
unexpended DRRMF to the STF bank account and ensure correct bank
account used for the payment of transactions.

7.16 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the CDLN
Administrator and School Accountant to properly monitor the expenditures,
post the unrecorded disbursements and transactions, and make necessary
adjustments to reflect accurate balances of the accounts in the financial
statements.

8.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the CDLN
Administrator and School Accountant to reconcile the accounts and prepare
the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts in the
financial statements.

9.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials
concerned to strictly monitor the project implementation to ensure
completion of the project by setting time frames and target dates so that
constituents could promptly avail of the benefits of the project and improve
the living and economic conditions of the constituents. Moreover, instruct
the Municipal Budget Officer to reflect the actual obligations/utilization in
the SAAOB to accurately report the total utilization and balances of the
fund.

10.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the CDLN School
Accountant to prepare and submit complete financial reports of the CDLN in
compliance with the provisions of the Manual on Setting Up and Operation
of LEE. Likewise, ensure completeness of signature and approval of proper
officials in the disbursement vouchers before payment to payees.

11.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the account balance, remit any unremitted GSIS
contributions and loan repayments, and henceforth, remit in full within the
prescribed due date all amounts withheld for each month to avoid
imposition of penalties.

12.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to remit any unremitted Pag-IBIG contributions and loan
repayments, and for the ensuing transactions to remit in full and on time
within the prescribed due date all amounts withheld for each month.

13.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a. Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the lacking Work
Suspension Orders, Work Resumption Orders, and time extension
approved by the LCE and indicate in the Certificate of Completion and
Acceptance the date of completion of the project;

b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims were adequately supported
with the required documentary requirements before payment to
contractors is processed; and

c. Local Finance Committee concerned to comply with the posting of the
quarterly report of LGSF-SBDP of the NTF-ELCAC pursuant to Local
Budget Circular No. 144 dated February 28, 2022.

14.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the HRMO and the
Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the
documentary requirements for the payment of terminal leave.

15.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to submit to the Auditor a
status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and
recommendations embodied in the Annual Audit Report using the
prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 204-002 dated March 18,
2014 and furnish copies of the said report to the agencies/offices
enumerated in Section 89 of the General Provisions of RA No. 11936.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account and record the
unexpended Quick Response and Mitigation Funds – MOOE for CY 2022 to
Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds and
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the account balance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to provide the lacking disclosures and to strictly adhere to the
disclosure requirements in the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure that
the financial reports are effectively and fully understood by the intended users.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to direct the Liquidating Officer and the
collecting clerks to prepare and submit the Report of Collections and
Deposits in a timely manner and report all transactions in the month and
year that they occur, and to submit the report to the accounting office;
and
b) The Municipal Accountant record in the books the unreported collections
of ₱585,922.89 accordingly.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention on the
preparation and submission of the BRS. The discrepancies in the BRS
which are validated by supporting documents shall be adjusted
immediately to correct the balances of Cash in Bank accounts and prepare
subsidiary ledgers per bank account;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to transfer the accounts
maintained with PNB to any of the accredited government servicing bank
accounts; and
c. Consider maintaining only one bank account for GF for local income
collections and IRA of the LGU, as well as for the trust fund accounts, for
a stronger internal control and convenience in the disbursement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list of RPT and SET taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
during the year; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET Receivables at the
beginning of the year and prepare adjusting entries to record prior years’
receivable to reflect the agency receivables from its taxpayers, and
maintain subsidiary ledgers to facilitate evaluation and monitoring of
accounts. In addition, effect the necessary adjustments on the errors to
correct the reported balances of the affected accounts in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (a) review and reconcile the balance of the Advances to
Contractors account and demand refund for unrecouped advance payments
to contractors, if any; and (b) maintain subsidiary ledgers by contractor and
monitor closely all payments made to avoid recurrence of overpayment to
contractors.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant conduct a
detailed review of the reciprocal accounts among the three funds and
determine the cause of the unreconciled balance. Thereafter, make the
necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to reflect the
correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in
recording and classifying the municipality’s inter-fund transactions to ensure
that the balances of reciprocal accounts are equal at all times.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries, and henceforth, record
the transactions to its proper accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to review and reconcile monthly taxes withheld to ensure that the entire
amount is remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within the
prescribed due date and prepare JEV to correct the affected accounts in the
books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to (a) require the collectors concerned to remit the collections and
present the unissued accountable forms, if any; (b) monitor the reported AFs
in the RCD against the issued AFs to the collectors to verify any
unrecorded/unremitted collections; and (c) strictly adopt the system of issuing
AF to collectors only upon full remittance of collections from the previous
booklet of AF issued.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (1) demand immediate refund of advance payments given to
contractors for completed projects and maintain subsidiary ledger per
contractor for the monitoring of payments made, and (2) ensure that the
advance payment of 15% of the contract price is supported with irrevocable
standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank
guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or
insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission prior to
payment to contractors.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of authorizing the
automatic transfer of funds from the General Fund bank account to the
calamity fund bank account and strictly observe the guidelines set forth in
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. We further
recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to maintain subsidiary ledgers by year based on source and on unspent DRRM
funds of LGUs from current and prior years’ appropriations.

We recommended that the management stop funding the payment of
honoraria out of the regular appropriations of the municipality. The amount
necessary should be taken from the proceeds from the sale of bid documents,
fees from contractor/supplier registry, fees charged for copies of minutes of
bid openings, BAC resolutions and other BAC documents, protest fees, other
proceeds from bid security forfeiture and from the agency’s savings after the
mandatory expenditure items have been satisfied with.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to adhere to the required
activities and timelines in the planning and budgeting for the SEF. The annual
SEF budget must be prepared and must conform to the schedule of activities
cited in Section 5.3 of DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular 01, s.2017 for
the benefit of all public schools within its jurisdiction.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. All JO personnel to submit their approved DTR and/or individual
accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate
supervisors to the HRMO or any official or employee assigned for the
purpose;
b. The official or employee assigned to furnish the Audit Team of the
approved Job Order/Contracts; and
c. The HRMO or employee assigned to verify the actual number of days
worked by the JO as basis in determining the amount of compensation to
be paid for the month. Likewise, to require all Job Order personnel, to use
the biometric machine and submit a copy of the individual attendance
record report generated by the biometric system for the establishment of a
reliable number of days worked. Also, to submit the travel orders and
certificate of appearances when on official business or on field
transactions. And, the conduct regular orientation/re-orientation at the
start of the contract period of Job Order employees, to discuss the basic
office rules and regulations and documentary requirements in the payment
of their wages.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that seminars
and conventions attended is responsive to the organizational result and
development outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial
resources of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards
Committee/BAC Secretariat/TWG to (a) post the advertisement,
supplemental bid bulletins, notice of award, approved contract and notice to
proceed at the PHILGEPS and at prescribed places, (b) submit proof of
invitation of three observers and (c) attach the plans/drawings and technical
specifications in the bidding documents.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (1)
submit to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements on various
COVID 19 related disbursements; and (b) ensure completeness of
documentation prior to processing of payment of claims against government
funds.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD FPS, Technical Working
Group (TWG) and secretariat to (a) fully implement GAD-related PPAs by
utilizing the whole amount set aside for each PPA so that the desired outcomes
are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest extent by the intended
beneficiaries, and (b) undergo capacity building programs on GAD for the
effective planning and implementation of GAD PPAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Local Finance
Committee ensure that the 20% DF shall be utilized for development projects
and refrain from charging ineligible expenditures enumerated in Item 3.2.5 of
DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Engineer to (i) inform the Contractors through written communication,
requiring them to submit written request for extension of contract time, citing
therein the reasons for the delay in project completion; (ii) examine the facts,
extent and reasonableness of the delay and recommend to the LCE the
approval or disapproval of the CTE; and (iii) if warranted, account for the
negative slippage and impose liquidated damages to the Contractor.

We recommended that Management ensure that development projects are
well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready to avoid delay in
the implementation and completion of the priority development projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Council
to properly plan, prioritize, and evaluate the programs, projects, and activities
to be funded out of the LDRMMF to attain the objective and purpose of the
Fund by observing the rules and regulations thereto. Moreover, we also
recommend that the council reassess the necessity of pursuing the
unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same to those PPAs, which
will enhance the disaster resilience and preparedness of communities with the
goal of promoting sustainable socio-economic growth.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the SAAOB for continuing
appropriations and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the
SCBAA. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to include in the Notes
to Financial Statements the Reconciliation Statement of Comparison of Budget
and Actual and the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the bank reconciliation statements for each account and maintain the
subsidiary ledgers of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current for CY 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that the cashbooks are handled and maintained
accordingly by adopting the following suggested measures:
a) Require the DO to update the cashbooks regularly, daily or monthly
whichever is practicable, based on the Report of Disbursement.
b) Direct the Disbursing Officer to maintain a separate cashbook for
payroll fund and for operating expenses in each fund.
c) Require the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor and ensure the
accuracy and physical existence of cash on hand with the cash
balance on the cashbook of all the accountable officers.
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger
for the Payroll Fund and Cash-In-Disbursing Officer in each fund.
e) Reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting record every
end of the month or at least quarterly.
f) Require the Disbursing Officers and the Municipal Accountant to
immediately take up necessary adjustments on the books to reflect
the correct cash balance and to reconcile with the accounting
records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor impose the rule of
immediately issuing an Official Receipt on all receipt of government
funds. Likewise, instruct the CDLN Administrator to require the
collecting officers of CDLN to appropriately replace the temporary
receipts issued, with an Official Receipt and/or those to which an OR
was not issued. Further, make a list of the ORs issued and post them
in conspicuous places within the CDLN premises with information that
the ORs can already be claimed in the Cashier’s Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the CDLN
Administrator to monitor the holding of collections in the hands of the
accountable officers and require them to deposit all collections daily or
upon reaching ₱5,000.00 to avoid using them for purposes other than
for deposit to its authorized government depository bank.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, require the
Municipal Treasurer to verify the reports submitted by the RCC with
the Official Receipts used, the logbook for the released AF to the RCC
and conduct the physical count of the AF in the hands of the collectors
at least every turn over and/or end of the month so as to ensure that all
collections are turned over and completely reported in the RCD.
Further, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the RCCs to
account and appropriately remit all collections and AFs in their
possession immediately.

We recommended that the LCE require:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to regularly deposit intact the collections
and any LGU funds in his possession in the authorized government
55
depository bank.
b) The Municipal Accountant to verify the report submitted by the
Municipal Treasurer with the supporting documents and the
reported AF used so as to ensure that all collections and revenues
are completely recorded, and
c) Both the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records to ensure the accuracy and fair presentation of the
Financial Reports.

We recommended that the LCE require the School Administrator to
turn over the checks in the custody of the CDLN to the Municipal
Treasurer as he is the authorized officer to draw all checks in
settlement of the LGU. Also, submit all the transactions of the CDLN
for certifications and/or approval of the authorized officials of the
LGU.

We recommended that the LCE require the School Administrator to
reconsider the merging of the accounts maintained by the CDLN.
However, if it will be impractical to do so, device measures to ensure
that all funds received and disbursed by the CDLN be monitored and
appropriately approved by the proper officials of the LGU.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive the following courses of
action:
a) Create an Inventory Committee tasked to prepare a methodical and well-
planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year-round
basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality have
been accounted for and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), thereon;
b) Direct the Property and Accounting Unit to undertake collaborative
procedures to ensure that all PPEs are incorporated in the RPCPPE,
including infrastructure assets; and
c) Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the process of the disposal of
unserviceable properties of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revert the amount reflected
in the Trust Liability-DRRMF account to the General Fund since the five-year
validity period of the unexpended balance from CY 2013, 2014 and 2015 has
already lapsed.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor and
ensure that all amounts obligated under the LDRRMF shall only be within the
approved appropriation to avoid incurrence of expenditures in excess of its
appropriated amount.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(MDRRMO) to systematically undertake the preparation of the LDRRMF
Investment Plan, taking into consideration the four thematic areas where the
70% LDRRM Fund should be allocated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board
to annually prepare SEF Budget in accordance to Republic Act No. 7160 and
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that claims for RATA are valid, proper and supported
with complete documentation.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Health Office (MHO)
personnel concerned to submit immediately to the Audit Team, through the
Municipal Accountant, the following documents:
a) Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/office order/authority to
report for work;
b) Daily Time Records with biometric printouts/copy of logbook or
Certificate of Attendance in the COVID-19 Facility/Center by their
immediate supervisor during the period of the claim; and
c) Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID-19 patients
attended to.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, through the Municipal
Accountant, be required to submit immediately the aforementioned lacking
supporting documents to the Audit Team.

Further, we recommended that the PESO Manager strictly adhere to the
guidelines set forth in the DOLE Department Order No. 219 Series of 2020
and ensure that the payment is valid, proper and reasonable.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as a result of our audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer, Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit
the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and
cancelled checks, if any. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal
Accountant ensure that all disbursement vouchers and their supporting
documents, copies of cancelled checks, and other transaction documents are
submitted timely and regularly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to render their accounts and submit the required financial
reports within the reglementary period. Henceforth, strictly observe Section
347 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of
financial accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the designated Supply
Officer of the RHU to (i) prepare monthly inventory report and stock cards
for each item; and (ii) prepare and maintain a Summary/List of donated
vaccines received, distributed and balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person
and Departments concerned to:
a) Endeavor to fully implement the programs/projects/activities contained in
its GAD Plan and Budget to realize its intended purposes; and
b) Adhere to the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget in the
implementation of the programs and utilization of funds incorporated
therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and other officials
concerned in the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of 20%
DF in accordance with the approved investment plans;
2. Municipal Engineer to administer, coordinate, supervise, and control the
construction engineering and public works projects of Las Navas,
including the implementation of barangay projects charged under 20% DF
for an immediate implementation of planned development projects; and
3. Municipal Accountant to demand the submission of the liquidation reports
from barangay chairman to liquidate the grant of Fund Transfers charged
under 20% DF.

1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor identify the staff in the
Accounting Office who can assist the designated Municipal Accountant to
prepare the required Financial Reports/Statements and its corresponding
schedules. Further, require the newly hired Municipal Accountant to
recheck the Financial Statements and effect any adjustments/corrections to
the errors and misstatements in the Financial Statements for CY 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to direct all collecting officers to regularly prepare Report of Collections and Deposits at the close of each business day and remit their collections daily, particularly when collection exceeds P5,000.00; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit the amount of collections on hand or in vault as prescribed; (c) Municipal Treasurer to accordingly supervise the collectors in the issuance of receipts in acknowledgment of collection (d) require the collecting officers and cashier of CDLN to submit the duplicate copies of unaccounted accountable forms enumerated in Item 3; and (e) require both the Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to ensure the completeness and reliability of the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC, through its Secretariat, ensure that Invitation or Request for submission of price quotations/proposals be posted in the PhilGEPS website and in the premises of the entity for at least three (3) calendar days.  Furthermore, require from bidders the submission of the technical documents before an award of the contract be made.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Finding No. 7 | The BAC was instructed to observe the procurement processes and reportorial requirements.
observations.reason: No reason provided by management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Heads of Office and/or end-user ensure that all the procurement needs of their office in completely and appropriately included in the PPMP.

Budget Office evaluate the PPMP and ensure that it include all the planned procurements of the different offices/end-users and include only those waranted procurement needs in the proposed budget for approval by the HOPE, and observe the submission of the PPMP to the BAC for consolidation.

BAC continuously review its procurement activities and prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in detail; ensure that all procurement activities to be undertaken are included in the plan; 

BAC, through its Secretariat, prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies of compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency thereof; and

The Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant, ensure that no payment or disbursement should be made for procurement which are not in accordance with the approved budget and APP.

observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Finding No. 15 | None
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report. | The CDLN Administra-tor insisted that the CDLN should not be considered as a Local Economic Enterprise, but as a part of the social obligation of the LGU to provide affordable education to its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, designate to adequately disclose the details of all the bank accounts maintained by the LGU and its individual balances and to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements together with the bank statements and endorsed checks.  We likewise recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the cancellation of stale checks and record the transaction as Accounts Payable and require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the encashment of checks issued and send a written notice to the payee at least one (1) month before a check becomes stale informing him/her the existence of check.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2017 Finding No. 1 | Bank Recon-ciliation for eleven bank account were prepared in CY 2018 and copies of bank statements were submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report. | Turn-over of the position of accountant and accounting staff left the preparation of the bank reconciliation incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, designate and Municipal Treasurer identify and prepare the necessary schedules with relevant information on the claimant and age of the claims and submit all the Disbursement Vouchers for validation of the recorded payables.  Otherwise, we recommend reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund all accounts which has been outstanding for more than 2 years and those which are not covered with actual claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the replacement of
the designated Municipal Accountant with personnel who has the
capabilities to perform the accounting works. Hiring of a consultant
should not be the solution as it will also violate provisions on the fidelity of
handling government documents. Instead, prioritize the posting and
hiring a permanent Municipal to ensure that the one who will take charge
of the accounting office possesses the minimum requirement for the
position.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to designate an in-charge
in the accounting office personnel who is well equipped on the
maintenance and preparation of the different accounting journals and
ledgers and require the Accountant-designate to observe and follow the
instructions prescribed in accomplishing the different journals and
ledgers. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
ICO-Municipal Accountant to submit the Journal Entry Voucher together
with necessary supporting documents of all the financial transactions
recorded in the Journals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal
Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers on the control accounts and
reconcile the balances of the subsidiary ledgers with the general ledger.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Budget Officer to:
a. Indicate the prescribed account codes for every Obligation Requests
(ObR) prepared;
b. Prepare the Registries for regular, special and continuing
appropriations in a manner prescribed in the NGAS Manual for
LGUs; and
c. Reconcile with the accounting office on the account codes used
following the prescribed codes in Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the ICO-
Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation
statements and provide adequate disclosure on the details of all bank
accounts maintained by the LGU and its individual balances. Henceforth,
require that the ICO-Municipal Accountant strictly observe the monthly
preparation and submission of the bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Assign one officer in charge with the duties of a General Services
Officer; and
b. Require the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to
submit the required reports so that the Municipal Accountant can
record all the road network owned by the municipality to ensure that
all roads are properly valued and recorded, and to assure the fair
presentation of the account infrastructure assets in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. ICO-Municipal Accountant to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger for the
“Due to NGAs” account under the General Fund and provide details
on the PPA, Source Agency (SA) and amounts received,
utilizations/disbursements for each PPA, and balance/excess funds for
the amount of ₱10,030,494.94. Reconcile any differences, if any;
b. ICO-Municipal Accountant to provide details on the PPA, Source
Agency (SA) and amounts received, utilizations/disbursements for
each PPA, and balance/excess funds for the Subsidiary Ledger
maintained under the Trust Fund, and reconcile the difference of
₱14,572,813.91 to effect the necessary adjustments for projects which
were already completed and correspondingly liquidated with the
source agency; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to refund the ₱886,300.00 to DTI.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Enforce the rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash
advances,
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue a Certification that the
previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the
books,
c. Regularly update the Subsidiary Ledgers of each employee for the
advances granted so as to ensure that no officer/employee/accountable
officer will be granted of additional cash advance unless the previous
cash advance granted was fully liquidated or settled,
d. Enforce the withholding of salary and all claims of employees to settle
their long outstanding cash advances, and
e. Consider requesting for write-off of the unliquidated cash advances of
accountable officials who are already dead or those who could no
longer be located.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Designate an employee to monitor the implementation of the recipient
barangays and schools to determine whether the funds transferred
were used as intended and/or to determine and address the reasons
for the non-implementation of the individual projects and to provide
technical assistance in the implementation of the PPAs;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant-designate to ensure complete
documentation of the disbursements before approval; and
c. Require the recipients of the fund transfers to submit the liquidation
and return any unused funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a. End-users to indicate in their respective PPMPs the activities to be
undertaken for the budget year, with details on the target number of
participants, number of days, and estimated cost per pax/day, and
submit to the Head of BAC Secretariat for consolidation.
Supplemental APP should also be submitted, when necessary;
b. Bids and Awards Committee to submit a justification on their non-
compliance with prescribed procedures in the procurement process
and submit the technical documents required from the supplier. Also,
submit the Purchase Orders/Contracts of Service on its ensuing
transactions together with other technical documents required in
COA Circular 2009-001 to the Audit Team within 5 days from
execution. Moreover, judiciously and meticulously plan the
procurement process of the LGU to ensure compliance with laws,
rules, and procedures; and
c. ICO-Municipal Accountant to strictly review its financial transactions
before certifying as to the completeness of supporting documents of
the claim, in compliance to R.A. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.
Moreover, require the submission of the lacking supporting
documents to the audit team immediately. Non-compliance may
subject the transactions to audit suspension.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a) Municipal Assessor to expedite the preparation/completion of the
updated assessment roll and forward the same to the Sangguniang
Bayan for the revision of the RPT assessment;
b) Municipal Treasurer to conduct intensive tax campaign and
information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes;
c) Enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax
campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax dues; and
d) Exert efforts in collecting delinquent real property taxes, impose the
remedies provided for, and exercise its taxing authority mandated
under the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. In issuing the authority to render overtime services, ensure that the
expected output, date of completion/submission of the report, and the
authorized compensation is specifically provided;
b. Consider to avail the compensatory time/day-off, in lieu of the
overtime pay or non-compensatory remuneration policy for overtime
services pursuant to CSC and DBM JC No. 2;
c. Do not authorize employees holding a position higher than a Division
Chief or with Salary Grade 24 and higher to render overtime services
with compensation;
d. Require the concerned officials to submit the DTR during the regular
work for the months of February and June 2019, accomplishment
report and overtime work plan in all the overtime compensation
claimed; and
e. Ensure that the rendition overtime services shall only be authorized
for works that are extremely necessary pursuant to the same circular.

We recommended for the compliance of the rules and regulations on the
use of vehicles and consumption of fuel prescribed under COA Circular
No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. Use the prescribed format of the
Driver’s Trip Ticket and instruct the Driver to fill up properly and
completely of the information required. Henceforth, prepare and submit
the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Fuel Consumption Report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the HRMO to
initiate the hiring of a Municipal Accountant following the rules and
regulations prescribed by the Civil Service Commission.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Local
Finance Committee to observe compliance on the mandatory
appropriations and limitations prescribed in RA 7160 in the preparation
of the Annual Budget of the LGU. Require the Budget Officer to effect the
necessary corrections prescribed by the Provincial Finance Committee
and/or Sangguninag Panlalawigan. Likewise, we recommended that the
Sangguniang Bayan exercise due diligence in the review and evaluation of
the Annual Regular and Supplemental Budget of the LGU in determining
compliance with statutory and administrative requirements.

We recommended that management work out the 10-year Solid Waste
Management Plan and ensure that the residual wastes from the facility
will not contribute to the contamination of water and put public health at
risk.

In addition, expedite the closure of the existing open dumpsite and
operation of the new disposal facility. Likewise, we recommended that
management strictly enforce the other relevant provisions of the said law
in order to protect the public health of the residents and the environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School
Board to actively participate in the preparation of the Annual SEF Budget
taking into consideration the consolidated needs and priority activities and
programs of all the schools within the municipality.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure the collection of amounts from the persons liable for
the settlement of the disallowed transactions. Likewise, require the
Municipal Accountant-designate to monitor the settlement of the persons
liable to ensure the recording of the receivable of disallowance with issued
NFD and its corresponding settlements.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant-designate and Municipal treasurer to recomputed the
installment payment scheme and require full settlement of the
disallowances other than personnel services in nature. Further, require
the Municipal Treasurer to withhold all payments due the officer or
employee with disallowances observing the limitations set in the General
Appropriations Act.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant-designate to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers on each
of the persons liable and require the Municipal Treasurer to inform in
writing the retired or separated officers and employees with unsettled
disallowances and require settlement of the same. Further, forward to the
audit team the actions taken to require settlement of the disallowed
amount.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the audit
team the accounts that were the subject in the Notices of Suspension so it
won’t mature into a disallowance pursuant to Section 82 of Presidential
Decree 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the CDLN
Administrator to prepare a correct and complete financial reports of the
CDLN to be submitted thru the Local Finance Committee for review and
assessment of the financial sustainability of the LEE and to decide on the
continued operation or to devise other options in providing accessible
quality education to its constituents.

we recommended that the LCE request from the Board of Trustees to
review all the ordinances and charter and effect ammendments on the
provisions that are inconsistent with the rules and regulations on the
Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises.

we recommended the following:
a. Require the College Administrator/President to prepare the FSSE
Report (Sec. 7.1 of the Manual) which:
 summarizes side by side the Semi Annual Financial Self-
Sufficiency Review (SAFSSR) findings for the past 10 semi-
annual periods;
 computes the cumulative totals of income and expenses for the
past 10 semi-annual periods;
 determines the financial self-sufficiency ratio of the LEE;
 provides an explanation or analysis of the LEEs financial self-
sufficiency situation; and
 recommends whether the LEE shall continue to operate and
related actions.
b. The LCE and Local Sanggunian shall use the FSSE findings as bases
on whether to continue, modify, expand, scale down, or close the
CDLN operations. The conditions on the continuance or shutting
down and the steps to be followed depending on the decision arrived
by the LGU are presented in Section 7 of the Manual on the operation
of LEEs.
c. The LCE and SB should, likewise, take into consideration the option
of creating an economic enterprise unit to be headed by a permanent
staff of the LGU for the supervision of LEEs. As the manual
specifically provided that all personnel of the LEE shall be hired on a
contractual/casual/job order basis. The prohibition on appointing
permanent employees is manifested in the provision of the manual
that the cashier, who shall take custody of the LEE finances, should
be properly deputized by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) The Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the
LGU projects in accordance with the plans and schedules of
implementation to establish the cost of unperformed works as at the
expiration of the contract time, and recommend imposition of
Liquidated Damages.
b) The ICO-Municipal Accountant to issue Order of Payment for
Liquidated Damages as determined and recommended by the
Engineer.
c) Henceforth, deduct the computed liquidated damages from any money
due or which may become due the contractor under the contract
and/or from the retention money or other securities posted by the
contractor, whichever is convenient to the Procuring Entity.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC disqualify bidder with unsatisfactory performance and
appropriately evaluate that the key personnel are not pledged/used in
other on-going projects that are not adjacent to each other;
b. Municipal Engineer closely monitor the simultaneous implementation
123
of several projects awarded to the same contractors to avoid
unjustified delays and recommend imposition of Liquidated Damages;
and
c. ICO-Municipal Accountant issue Order of Payment for Liquidated
Damages as determined and recommended by the Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer closely monitor and
oversee the implementation of the projects sourced out thru fund transfers
from NGAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
MPDC/MAO/MSWDO to re-assess the viability of the project, submit any
modifications to DTI for review, and prioritize its implementation.
Otherwise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unused funds to
the source agency as provided in the MOA that the agreement shall be
terminated in the event the situations/circumstances would make it
impossible for the program to be carried out.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Ensure the availability of cash for the immediate release of the

b. Instruct the MSWDO to prioritize the preparation of the assessment

c. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to

d. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all AICS are supported with
complete supporting documents.
e. Require the Disbursing Officer to immediately liquidate the cash
advance granted to him per check number 1714310 amounting to
₱205,983.00 pursuant to Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445
and Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive revisit the Memorandum
of Agreement (MOA) entered into by and between the LGU and the
respective private lending institution. We further recommended to
include provisions in the MOA requiring the lending institutions the
payment of reasonable service fee to compensate for the expenses incurred
and man-hours utilized by the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a)  require  the
Municipal Treasurer to direct all collecting officers to  regularly
prepare Report of Collections and Deposits at  the  close  of  each
business day and remit their collections daily,  particularly  when
collection exceeds P5,000.00; (b) require the  Municipal  Treasurer
to strictly deposit the amount of collections on hand or  in  vault
as prescribed; (c) Municipal Treasurer to accordingly supervise the
collectors  in  the  issuance  of  receipts  in  acknowledgment  of
collection (d) require the collecting officers and cashier of  CDLN
to submit the duplicate copies  of  unaccounted  accountable  forms
enumerated in Item 3; and (e) require both the Accountant  and  the
Municipal Treasurer  to  reconcile  their  records  to  ensure  the
completeness and reliability of the reports.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  MHO  to
furnish the copy  of  delivery  receipt/Acceptance  and  Inspection
Report of all  received  medicines  and  medical  supplies  to  the
Accounting Office  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  record  all
inventory items acquired thru purchase and donation in its  related
inventory account Likewise, the  conduct  of  inventory  taking  of
supplies be made semestrally as mandated under Sec. 124 of the  New
Government Accounting System.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  MHO  to
install, implement and monitor the storage and distribution/use  of
medicines and medical equipment to avoid wastage.  The  RHU  should
keep records of each medicine with information  of  the  expiration
dates and running balances of  the  medicines  in  stock  to  avoid
wastage.  Expired medicines and unserviceable equipment  should  be
properly  segregated   to   avoid   contamination   and   risk   of
administering expired medicines and use of damaged equipment.

We recommended that a biometric machine be provided for the LGUs
employees to accurately  register  the  actual  office/work  hours,
require the submission of the DTRs based on the  biometric  machine
entries/records, and require each head of  office  to  monitor  the
attendance/absences of the personnel under his supervision.  In the
meantime, the HRMO should review or scrutinize  the  submitted  DTR
that it is the actual log-in and log-out of office of  an  employee
based on the logbook to arrive at a correct manhours or attendance.
 Likewise, the head of office  concerned  should  ensure  that  the
office hours reflected in the DTR is the actual hours  an  employee
is in the office, while, those on field, travel or on leave  should
be supported with an  approved  travel  order  and  certificate  of
appearance or approved application for leave.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  require  the  HRMO  to
submit a record of the JO employees detailing the expected work  to
be performed and/or output and require each payee  to  prepare  and
submit the accomplishment  report  duly  signed  by  the  immediate
supervisor.  We further recommended that the Local Chief  Executive
instruct the HRMO to prepare the  manning  schedule,  specifically,
the laborers/cleaners and ensure that the daily time record reflect
the actual hours worked by an employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-designate reconcile
the amount recorded in the financial records of the LGU  with  that
of the audit team  per  NFDs  received  and  effect  the  necessary
correction.  Moreover, observe the  rules  and  regulation  on  the
recording of disallowances  and  settlements  thereof  pursuant  to
Sections 22.6 and 24 of the Rules and Regulation on the  Settlement
of Accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  the  HRMO
to furnish the copies of the employment records and  reports  or  a
list of the employees covered by GSIS, HDMF  and  PHIC.   Likewise,
officers responsible in the preparation of the  payroll  should  be
required to enforce the mandatory deduction of the employee’s share
and remit the same including the government share to the respective
insurance fund.

We recommended that the BAC,  through  its  Secretariat,  ensure
that   Invitation   or   Request   for    submission    of    price
quotations/proposals be posted in the PhilGEPS website and  in  the
premises of the entity  for  at  least  three  (3)  calendar  days.
Furthermore, require from bidders the submission of  the  technical
documents before an award of the contract be made.

We recommended that the:

a) Heads of Office and/or end-user ensure that all the  procurement
needs of their office is completely and  appropriately  included
in the PPMP.


b) Budget Office evaluate the PPMP and ensure that it  include  all
the planned procurements of the different offices/end-users  and
consider only those waranted procurement needs in  the  proposed
budget for approval by the HOPE, and observe the  submission  of
the PPMP to the BAC for consolidation.


c) BAC continuously review its procurement activities  and  prepare
the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in  detail;  ensure  that  all
procurement activities to be  undertaken  are  included  in  the
plan;


d) BAC, through its Secretariat, prepare the Procurement Monitoring
Report (PMR) and submit the same to the GPPB in  the  prescribed
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every
semester,  as  required,  to  allow  monitoring  by  appropriate
government Oversight Bodies of compliance with the provisions of
the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency  thereof;
and


e) The Municipal Budget Officer  and  Accountant,  ensure  that  no
payment or disbursement should be made for procurement which are
not in accordance with the approved budget and APP.

We  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  Budget   Officer,
Treasurer,  and  Accountant  to  refrain  from  appropriating   and
disbursing local funds without legal basis to safeguard  the  funds
from wastage.  Likewise, require the LGU officers and employees  to
refund the amount received.

We recommended that sufficient supporting documents be submitted
to ascertain the nature  of  the  transaction.   Likewise,  clearly
establish/state  the  nature  of  payment  in  the  DVs  and  other
documents  submitted  considering  that  the  required   supporting
documents and rules and regulations applied differs in each type of
disbursement/expense.

We recommended that the MSWDO immediately  submit  the  requests
for assistance in times  of  disaster  and  closely  coordinate  to
partner agency, DSWD  RO-8,  to  fast  track  the  release  of  any
funds/assistance.  Likewise,  we  recommended  that  the  Municipal
Treasurer ensure the full implementation/pay-out of the  funds  and
limit the documents  required  to  what  is  necessary.   Likewise,
uniform application on the imposition of  documentary  requirements
should be observed.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended  that  the
Local Chief Executive:

a. Require the Municipal Engineer  to  inspect  the  water  system,
identify and evaluate any  defects  to  appropriately  recommend
appropriate measure to maximize the use of this infrastructure;


b. Ensure that the  necessary  permit  to  construct  the  birthing
facility is granted before pursuing the rehabilitation/repair to
avoid additional wastage of government funds; and


c. Require the Municipal Engineer and the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
furnish the Municipal Accountant-designate all the  records  and
documents relative to the construction and any rehabilitation on
these  infrastructure  to  facilitate  the  adjustment  of   the
valuation and proper accounting recognition of the assets.

We recommended that the LCE  require  the  MPDO  and  LDRRMO  to
incorporate in the  LDRRMO  Plan  and  Budget  provisions  for  the
stockpiling of  relief  goods  and  procurement  of  early  warning
device, rescue tools and equipment to immediately  respond  to its
constituents at times of disaster/calamity in the attainment of the
objective of RA 10121.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that:

a. The financial reports, assessment data should be used  as  basis
by the governing board of  CDLN  in  its  decision  in  devising
measure  to  achieve  the  objective  of  self-sufficiency   and
effectiveness.  Likewise, the limitations  of  operating  a  LEE
should be taken into consideration, bearing  in  mind  the  LGUs
developmental  goal,  objectives  and  services  that   can   be
effectively discharge out of the funds extended to  the  LEE  to
continue operation;


b. Adopt cost-cutting measures to lessen  expenditures  and devise
mechanism to boost revenue; and


c. Revisit the ordinances and charter and ensure that no provisions
therein are contrary to rules and  regulations  particularly  RA
7160 and the Manual on the Setting Up  and  Operation  of  Local
Economic Enterprises.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC and
Finance Committee to submit the  Local  Development  and  Financial
Plan  specifying  therein  the  specific  activities  and  expected
results of the programs to  ensure  that  the  use  of  funds  will
directly benefit the public.  Also, require the HRMO to prepare the
manning schedule with  information  of  the  work  assignments  and
functions of every Job Order employees to  prevent  duplication  of
task between them.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Accounting Office to properly identify and classify the accountable officers responsible in the liquidation/refund of the balances of advances accounts, submit the supporting details/schedules together with the prescribed subsidiary ledgers and to provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Note to FS. Likewise, require the full liquidation and refund/return of any unused/unutilized advances. These audit recommendation s is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Disallowance and Suspension on the validity of the payment of incentives and the completeness of documents submitted for the recorded liquidations and outright recording of expense despite absence of complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the  Municipal
   Accountant-designate to adequately disclose the details of all  the  bank
   accounts maintained by the LGU and its individual balances and to prepare
   and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements together with the  bank
   statements and endorsed checks.  We likewise  recommend  that  the  Local
   Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a  journal  entry
   voucher to take up the  cancellation  of  stale  checks  and  record  the
   transaction as Accounts Payable and require the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
   monitor the encashment of checks issued and send a written notice to  the
   payee at least one (1) month  before  a  check  becomes  stale  informing
   hin/her the existence of check.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Accounting Office
   to properly identify and classify the accountable officers responsible in
   the liquidation/refund of the balances of advances accounts,  submit  the
   supporting details/schedules  together  with  the  prescribed  subsidiary
   ledgers and to provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the  Note  to
   FS.  Likewise, require the full  liquidation  and  refund/return  of  any
   unused/unutilized advances.


        These audit recommendations is without prejudice to the issuance of
   Notices of Disallowance and Suspension on the validity of the payment  of
   incentives and the completeness of documents submitted for  the  recorded
   liquidations  and  outright  recording  of  expense  despite  absence  of
   complete supporting documents.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to  instruct  the  Municipal
   Accountant-designate to send demand letters, forward the  same  to  COA
   for the issuance of final demand and to strictly enforce the guidelines
   on the grant and liquidation of cash advances.  Also, enforce  the  with-
   holding of salaries of officers and employees with due unliquidated cash
   advances and initiate the filing of cases pursuant to Article 217 of  the
   Revised Penal Code.


      This audit recommendation is without prejudice of the issuance of  the
   Notice of Disallowance or Suspension.

We recommend  that  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Inventory
   Committee to conduct  annual  physical  inventory  and  render  a  report
   thereon.  Further, determine  the  fair  value  of  the  real  properties
   acquired through donation and record these in the books in accordance  to
   PPSAS 17.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, designate and Municipal
   Treasurer identify and prepare  the  necessary  schedules  with  relevant
   information on the claimant and age of the  claims  and  submit  all  the
   Disbursement  Vouchers  for  validation   of   the   recorded   payables.
   Otherwise, we recommend reversion to the unappropriated  surplus  of  the
   general fund all accounts which has been  outstanding  for  more  than  2
   years and those which are not covered with actual claims.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the  concerned
   officers to maintain and update the different registries and ledger cards
   and ensure the submission of  the  Monthly  Report  on  the  Receipt  and
   Distribution/Utilization/Distribution  of   Relief   Goods.    We   also,
   recommend that the Budget Officer, Accountant and LDRRMO  coordinate  and
   reconcile the amounts reflected in the reports prepared and maintained by
   their corresponding offices.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive  ensure  compliance  to
   the provisions of RA 10121, instruct the Budget Officer, MPDC and  LDRRMO
   Officer to review and effect the necessary adjustments in the LDRRM  Plan
   and Budget, and the Monthly Report of Receipt and Utilization  of  Funds.
   Likewise, we recommend that the LCE ensure the  immediate  implementation
   of identified LDRRM projects so that the people of Las Navas be  afforded
   of a calamity ready community.

We  recommend  that  the   Local   Chief   Executive   ensure   the
   implementation of the GAD Plan and  Budget  to  ensure  gender-responsive
   governance.  Furthermore, require the GFPS to ensure  the  assessment  of
   gender-responsiveness  of  programs  based  on  the  priority  needs   of
   constituents pursuant to PCW, NEDA and DBM JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Council  for  the  Protection  of
   Children be established to propose  and  implement  PPAs  that  advocates
   child  rights,   to   plan   and   conduct   monitoring   of   children’s
   programs/activities.   We  further  recommend  that  the  1%  of  IRA  be
   appropriated for the LCPC programs/activities as required under RA 9344.

We  recommend  that  management  create  a  Municipal  Solid  Waste
   Management Board and  prepare  a  10-year  Solid  Waste  Management  Plan
   consistent with the National Solid Waste  Management  Framework  as  been
   provided  for  in  Sections  12,  16  and  17  of  RA  9003  and  submit
   the  same  to  the  National  Solid  Waste  Management  Commission   for
   approval.   Moreover, ensure implementation of the same.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct  the  HRMO  to
   prepare the Duties and Responsibilities detailing  the  work  assignments
   and  functions  of   every   permanent   employee   to   prevent   hiring
   contractual/job order employee that will perform the job  description  of
   regular employees.  Also, adjust the rates of the JOs and/or the  regular
   employees observing  the  rates  prescribed.   Furthermore,  require  the
   contractual  employee  to  turn-over  and/or  submit  to  the   Municipal
   Accountant, designate all the required  deliverables  enumerated  in  the
   contract.

We recommend that the Local  Chief  Executive  instruct  the  Local
   Finance Committee to  appropriately  allocate  the  expenditures  in  the
   Annual  Budget  of  the  LGU  observing  compliance  on  the  limitations
   prescribed in RA 7160.  Likewise, we recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan
   exercise due diligence in the  review  and  determining  compliance  with
   statutory and administrative  requirements  of the annual regular and
   supplemental budget of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local School Board  actively  participate  in
   the preparation of the budget of SEF which should be supported  with  the
   budget requests from all the school heads of the municipality.  Likewise,
   the Municipal Accountant should ensure  that  complete  documentation  be
   attached in all the disbursements, that to include the liquidation report
   for the fund transfers duly verified by the assigned  auditor  of  DepEd.
   Also, require a certification  that  there  was  no  budgetary  provision
   intended for the related expenditures in the funds of DepED  and/or  fund
   received from other sources intended for the same purpose before granting
   the assistance.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require  the  Municipal
   Accountant, designate to prepare and submit  financial  reports  of  the
   CDLN to  be  submitted  thru  the  Local  Finance  Committee  for  review
   and assessment the financial sustainability of the  LEE  and  to decide
   on the continued operation or to devise other options  in  providing
   accessible quality education to its constituents.

We recommend that management determine and address the reasons  for
   the non-implementation of the individual projects and expedite  its
   implementation.  Likewise, in subsequent  appropriation,  recommend for the
   reversion  of  the  allocated  funds  for  the  unimplemented  ineligible
   projects/program to purposes outlined in the DBM &  DILG  Joint  Memorandum
   Circular  No.  2017-1.   Also,  submit  the  aforementioned  disbursement
   vouchers with its related supporting documents.

Moreover, create the inventory committee to  conduct the  physical count  of Inventories and PPE and  submit the  reports thereon.  Moreover,  require the  recognition of depreciation on the asset  accounts by the Accountant.
