Sangguniang Bayan for the creation of the required LDRRM Office and
provision of appropriation for its salaries and related expenses to carry out the
mandatory function of the office. Meanwhile, require the designated LDRRMO
to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM fund.
Furthermore, we recommend that the LCE require the MSWDO to prepare
and submit the Registry of Relief Goods to determine accountability and ensure
proper management. Likewise, submit the lacking documents for the payments
of handling and hauling of relief goods.

We recommend that management ensure that all disbursements charged
against the LDRRM Fund are in accordance with Section 1, Rule 18 of
Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management create a Solid Waste Management
Board to formulate Solid Waste Management Plan pursuant to Sections 12 & 16
of RA 9003. The plan should be in accordance with the components listed in
Section 17 of the same Act and ensure the implementation of the same.

We recommend that the BAC submit the lacking supporting documents.
Henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions in the procurement of
goods and services as outlined in the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and to
comply with the mandatory supporting documents and submission of
contracts and P.O. within five (5) working days from perfection thereof.

We recommend that the BAC strictly observe the procurement processes
as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of
R.A. 9184 and to submit the lacking procurement documents. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post-audit of the
documents required herein.

We recommend that the Local School Board actively participate in the
preparation of the Annual School Budget in accordance with Section 99(a)
of RA 7160 and strictly observe the rules and regulations on the utilization
of the SEF as provided for in Section 100(c) of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM,
DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s.1998. Henceforth, Section 4(6) of PD 1445
should be strictly adhered to.

We recommend that the salary schedule in CY 2017 be revised adopting
the rates of a 3rd class municipality and require the officers and employees to
refund the amount of excess salary, mid-year and year-end benefits totaling
P2,646,834.75. Further, strictly observe the guidelines on the computation of
salary step increment.

We recommend that above mentioned lacking documents be submitted.
Likewise, require the recipient officials and employees to refund the PEI in
excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in E.O. No. 80 and DBM BC No.
2016-8.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel officer to
immediately submit the lacking documents, and to require concerned
officials to strictly observe the rules and regulations as well as the
documentary requirements in processing application for monetization of
leave credits.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant strictly
observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA
7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009 to avoid any administrative disciplinary action.

We recommend that management prepare the Capacity Development
Agenda and the Local Chief Executive should ensure that seminars and
conventions attended is responsive to the organizational result and development
outcome, keeping in mind the prudent use of the scarce financial resources of
the LGU. Likewise, demand from the leagues of elected officials to
acknowledge payment thru AF 51.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to immediately submit the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents including cancelled/unaccounted checks. Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with the pertinent provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of audit of the documents submitted after the issuance of this memorandum.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the some of DVs.
observations.reason: Cancelled checks and Notices of Suspension remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe Section 14 of the revised IRR of RA 8291, on the remittance of monthly contributions due to GSIS to avoid penalties and surcharges. Moreover, the submission of the disbursement voucher and supporting documents for the remittance of interest due to late remittance amounting ₱8,763.82 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Observed the timely remittance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor immediately fill-up the Municipal Accountant position in accordance with Section 474 of RA 7160. Meanwhile, require the Municipal Accountant – Designate to perform the functions relative to the position and to strictly observe accounting and reporting rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Designated a CPA for the position work experience.
observations.reason: Lacked the 3year experience requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create and strengthen the GAD Focal Point System, establish the GAD data base, prepare and submit the GAD Plan and the Accomplishment Report, in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point Person designated.
observations.reason: GAD Focal Point Person has been designated. Reiterated in AAR 2013 and prior AARs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU ensure the submission of the LDRRMF Investment Plan and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer has already been designated and appointed in CY 2017.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Fund Plan and the Annual Budget were submitted and the responsibility in preparing the utilization information was on the Budget Officer and Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon, in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The committee has been created and issued a memorandum creating the inventory committee.
observations.reason: Despite the creation of the committee the inventory taking has not been conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management submit the lacking documents. Henceforth, comply the documentary requirements for fund transfer as provided for in Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Some fund transfers were supported with MOA. Submitted the lacking documents. Henceforth, comply with the documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Some transfers were supported with MOA. Lack of reports reflecting the balances of the fund transfers to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 86-255 as amended by COA Circular No. 95-011, Section 5.6 of COA Circular 2012-001 and the pertinent provision of the General Appropriations Act on the hiring of lawyers and grant of honorarium/allowance.
observations.managementAction: The private lawyer has been terminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6, dated November 7, 1975 and Section 4(2) of PD 1445 in order to control and regulate the use of government vehicles, and to ensure that the fuel consumed is for government use. Likewise, require the drivers concerned to submit Driver's Trip Tickets with the Monthly Report of Official Travels, and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of post-audit. We further recommend that a cut-off date for submission of unpaid claims prior to the close of the fiscal year be established so that these claims will be recorded during the year.
observations.managementAction: DTRs were attached to the payments of salaries and wages.
observations.reason: No assigned employee to prepare the monthly reports and monitor the purchases and usage of gasoline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU exercise fully its taxing power as authorized under Section 142 to augment the funding necessary for the operation of the municipality and implement austerity measures to reduce operating expenses and utilize internal revenue fund for developmental projects that would be beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Revenue Code of 2014 has been adopted to increase generation of income.
observations.reason: Difficulty in collection of RPT taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to Section 12, Item C of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF was prepared together with the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: The submission of the responsibility in preparing the utilization information was upon the Budget Officer and Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The delayed time in the submission of accounts had been minimized in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU exercise transparency and accountability on all projects/programs/activities and submit the list of projects/programs/activities to COA.
observations.managementAction: Management posted the list in the bulletin board in the office.
observations.reason: Management posted the list but failed to submit a copy to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU ensure that all expenditures should be in conformity with the approved budget by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and the corresponding Local Budget Circular provisions. Require refund of excess salaries pursuant to applicable circulars.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer maintained that they are authorized to adopt the rate of a 1st class municipality in consideration of the Sangguniang Bayan ordinance.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report; the SB authorization and SP enactment viewed as operative.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of Executive Order No. 298 and COA Circular No. 96-004. Require officers and employees concerned to refund the amounts disallowed in audit. Ensure proper documentation and compliance with travel and reimbursement rules.
observations.managementAction: DTRs were attached to the payments of salaries and wages.
observations.reason: The SASDC as of Dec. 31, 2016 did not reflect balance of disallowance on excess payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management remit to the BIR the amounts of taxes withheld from suppliers in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 14-2003 and to maintain subsidiary ledgers for tax withheld per supplier to facilitate easy monitoring of remittances.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.
observations.reason: No document was provided to show that the taxes withheld has been remitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our prior year recommendation that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184 on alternative mode of procurement for supplies and materials and that procurement above the threshold should be done through competitive public bidding.
observations.managementAction: Public Bidding was observed by the LGU for those procurements above the threshold.
observations.reason: Procurements above the threshold are done through public bidding; some smaller procurements continued under alternative mode.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and concerned officials of the municipality must strictly observe Section 88 of PD No. 1445 with respect to the payment of salaries and wages of government employees to avoid consequences that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: DTRs were attached to the payments of salaries and wages.
